subtopics.title: Nature and Importance of Chemistry
common_misconceptions: Chemistry is only about dangerous chemicals
Chemistry has no relevance to daily life
subtopics.teaching_note: Show how chemistry applies to cooking, medicine, agriculture, and industry. Emphasize laboratory safety rules.

subtopics.title: Laboratory Techniques and Apparatus
common_misconceptions: Confusing different separation techniques
Not understanding when to use each technique
subtopics.teaching_note: Demonstrate each separation technique with real examples. Explain the principle behind each method.

subtopics.title: Fundamental Particles of the Atom
common_misconceptions: Confusing atomic number with mass number
Thinking isotopes have different chemical properties
subtopics.teaching_note: Use the mass number and atomic number to calculate subatomic particles. Emphasize that isotopes have identical chemical properties.

subtopics.title: Atomic Models and Electron Configuration
common_misconceptions: Electrons orbit the nucleus like planets
Not understanding the maximum electrons per shell
subtopics.teaching_note: Explain that the Bohr model is a simplification. Use the 2n² rule for maximum electrons and demonstrate electron configuration for elements 1-20.

subtopics.title: Periodic Law and Classification
common_misconceptions: Confusing periods with groups
Thinking periodic table order is based on atomic mass
subtopics.teaching_note: Emphasize that the table is organized by atomic number. Show trends across periods and down groups.

subtopics.title: Periodic Trends
common_misconceptions: Confusing ionization energy with electron affinity
Not understanding the relationship between trends and electron configuration
subtopics.teaching_note: Relate periodic trends to atomic structure and electron shielding. Use graphs to illustrate trends.

subtopics.title: Ionic (Electrovalent) Bonding
common_misconceptions: Thinking ionic compounds form molecules
Not understanding the difference between ions and atoms
subtopics.teaching_note: Use dot-and-cross diagrams to show electron transfer. Emphasize the formation of giant ionic lattices, not molecules.

subtopics.title: Covalent and Metallic Bonding
common_misconceptions: Thinking covalent bonds always share electrons equally
Not understanding metallic bonding explains conductivity
subtopics.teaching_note: Compare and contrast the three bonding types. Use molecular models for covalent compounds. Explain metallic bonding using the 'sea of electrons' model.

subtopics.title: The Mole Concept and Avogadro's Constant
common_misconceptions: Confusing molar mass with relative molecular mass
Not understanding that the mole is a counting unit
subtopics.teaching_note: Use the analogy of a dozen to explain the mole. Practice calculations converting between mass, moles, and number of particles.

subtopics.title: Chemical Equations and Calculations
common_misconceptions: Not balancing equations before calculations
Using wrong mole ratios from unbalanced equations
subtopics.teaching_note: Emphasize that equations must be balanced before any stoichiometric calculations. Use step-by-step mole method for calculations.

subtopics.title: Kinetic Theory of Matter
common_misconceptions: Particles in solids don't move
Gas particles have no volume
subtopics.teaching_note: Use demonstrations and simulations to show particle behavior in different states.

subtopics.title: Gas Laws
common_misconceptions: Confusing Boyle's and Charles's laws
Not converting temperature to Kelvin
subtopics.teaching_note: Always convert temperature to Kelvin. Use graphical representations and practical demonstrations.
